title: nephelium glabrum
intro: Nephelium glabrum, also known as the Rambutan tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae, native to Borneo. The plant's medicinal properties are primarily due to its seeds, which contain a variety of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenes. The seeds have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, diarrhea, and digestive issues, thanks to their anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, which provide benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Nephelium glabrum seeds can be prepared into herbal remedies through methods like decoction, infusion, or encapsulation, allowing for easy consumption and absorption of their medicinal properties.
